summary: Updated tools/frank.py so its instruction-swap heuristic treats PowerPC fdivs like li/lfs in the specific three-instruction swap pattern. The PR body says this fixed Player_80032BB0 in src/melee/pl/player.c, but did not eliminate the player.c Makefile kludge because Player_GetPtrForSlot was still broken by frank.

key_files.role: Modified frank's opcode-swap logic to include FDIVS in an existing opcode_b whitelist.

what_changed: Added an FDIVS opcode constant in tools/frank.py as 0xEC >> 2.
Expanded an existing conditional from opcode_b in [LI, LFS] to opcode_b in [LI, LFS, FDIVS].
Kept the existing swap-shape requirements intact: vanilla_inst_a == profile_inst_b, vanilla_inst_b == profile_inst_a, and vanilla_inst_c == profile_inst_c.
According to the PR body, this fixed frank-related breakage for Player_80032BB0 in src/melee/pl/player.c.
The PR body explicitly notes that the player.c Makefile kludge could not yet be removed because Player_GetPtrForSlot remained broken by frank.
smart_moves: The fix was narrowly scoped to frank's opcode whitelist instead of changing the broader matching/swap structure.
FDIVS was encoded consistently with the surrounding opcode constants by shifting the primary opcode byte right by 2.
The author documented both the immediate win, Player_80032BB0, and the remaining limitation, Player_GetPtrForSlot, avoiding overclaiming that the Makefile workaround was obsolete.
decomp_lessons: When a decomp tooling heuristic swaps nearby instructions, audit whether the whitelist covers all instruction classes that can legally participate in the same scheduling pattern.
A single missing floating-point opcode in tooling can keep otherwise-correct C from matching, especially in functions with instruction-order sensitivity.
Do not remove repository-level kludges just because one affected function starts matching; verify all functions covered by the workaround first.
For frank.py-style fixes, preserve existing equality checks around the swap unless there is evidence the whole pattern is wrong.
naming_conventions: PowerPC opcode constants in tools/frank.py use uppercase mnemonic names such as ADDI, LI, LMW, and FDIVS.
The code treats LI as an alias of ADDI with RA=0, documented inline as `LI = ADDI`.
Affected player symbols mentioned in the PR body use project-style names such as Player_80032BB0 and Player_GetPtrForSlot.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: FDIVS was added as `0xEC >> 2`, matching the existing style where opcode constants are derived from the top opcode bits.
The relevant frank heuristic detects a specific adjacent instruction swap where instruction A and B exchange positions while instruction C remains unchanged.
Including FDIVS in `opcode_b in [LI, LFS, FDIVS]` allows frank to recognize swap cases involving single-precision floating-point division.
review_feedback: No line review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR was merged after one recorded review, but the slice does not include review text or rationale.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for Player_GetPtrForSlot and frank to find when the remaining player.c Makefile kludge was resolved.
Check tools/frank.py history for additional opcode whitelist expansions around swap heuristics.
Audit other floating-point instructions near unmatched functions to see whether frank handles their scheduling patterns.
